topic.what: First experimental implementation of FHE-AUX-QHE framework on real IBM Quantum hardware
topic.how: Implemented complete AUX-QHE protocol with five phases (key generation, encryption, homomorphic evaluation, decryption, error mitigation) on three circuit configurations (5q-2t, 4q-3t, 5q-3t)
topic.why_matters: Bridges gap between theoretical QHE frameworks and practical NISQ deployment; provides concrete benchmarks for noise mitigation and establishes resource thresholds for practical deployment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Output correctness vs unencrypted circuit in ideal simulation
characteristics.evidence: Table I shows perfect fidelity across all 3 configurations in simulation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Output correctness on IBM Quantum ibmtorino (133 qubits)
characteristics.evidence: Table II shows 62-88% fidelity loss on real hardware across configurations
characteristics.context: significant noise degradation; 5q-2t best at 0.3569, 5q-3t worst at 0.1199

characteristics.what_itasures: Hardware fidelity after Opt-3+ZNE error mitigation
characteristics.context: Combined mitigation yields consistent improvements; 4q-3t shows mitigation overhead causes degradation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of pre-generated auxiliary qubits required
characteristics.context: Primary scalability bottleneck; 54x growth from 5q-2t to 5q-3t

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cross-term count in symbolic QOTP key polynomials
characteristics.context: Rapid growth with T-depth; layer sizes grow exponentially (10,105) to (10,105,6090)

characteristics.evidence: Table I Section C shows stable ~0.031s across all configurations
characteristics.context: Confirms AUX maintains compact decryption independent of circuit complexity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to pre-generate all auxiliary states
characteristics.context: Grows linearly with auxiliary count; reaches 90% of total runtime at 5q-3t

good_for: assessing security-accuracy tradeoffs in practical QHE

novelty: First real quantum hardware implementation of FHE-AUX-QHE scheme
Quantified NISQ noise impact on AUX-QHE performance
Established practical resource thresholds (31025 aux states, 5505 cross-terms)
Compared error mitigation strategies (ZNE, Opt-3, combined) on QHE
open_problems: Exponential auxiliary-state growth limits scalability beyond T-depth 3
Mitigation overhead can exceed benefits at higher T-depths
Integration of quantum error correction (QEC) needed for secure computation
State reuse and T-gadget compression needed for practical AUX-QHE
